Lash Out.chat: [[lookup @name]] lashes out with a fast burst of deathly power.

Shadow Veil.name: Shadow Veil
Shadow Veil.description: [[lookup @name]] gives herself the &Reference[Invisible apply=false] condition until the start of her next turn and can fly up to half her Fly Speed. The condition ends early immediately after [[lookup @name]] deals damage. Saidra can’t take this action again until the start of her next turn.
Shadow Veil.chat: A veil of shadow gathers around [[lookup @name]] as she fades from view.

Invisible.name: Invisible
Invisible.description: Saidra gives herself the &Reference[Invisible apply=false] condition until the start of her next turn and can fly up to half her Fly Speed.

Shape-Shift.name: Shape-Shift
Shape-Shift.description: [[lookup @name]] shape-shifts into the Red Death, or she returns to her true form as the duchess. Her game statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment she is wearing or carrying isn’t transformed.
Shape-Shift.chat: [[lookup @name]]’s outline starts to alter as she shifts into a different form.

Darklord Restoration.name: Darklord Restoration
Darklord Restoration.description: If [[lookup @name]] dies, she revives with all her Hit Points in [[/r 1d10]] weeks somewhere in Dementlieu.

Truth Or Die.name: Truth Or Die
Truth Or Die.description: [[lookup @name]] asks a creature she can see within 60 feet to identify itself and casts [[/item .2wD46WQ1fYXhIWJw]]{Disintegrate}, targeting the creature, requiring no Material components and using [[lookup @attributes.spell.abilityLabel]] as the spellcasting ability (DC [[lookup @attributes.spell.dc]], with Disadvantage if the target lies).
Truth Or Die.chat: With a calm, exacting tone, [[lookup @name]] asks the target to identify itself as deadly magic gathers around her.

Lie Detector.name: Lie Detector
Lie Detector.description: [[lookup @name]] knows whether she hears a lie.

Life Drain.name: Life Drain
Life Drain.description:
Constitution Saving Throw: DC [[lookup @save.dc.value activity=iiG6ykiiavc0o4qo]], one creature [[lookup @name]] can see within [[lookup @range.value activity=iiG6ykiiavc0o4qo]] feet.

Failure: [[/damage average]] damage, and its Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the damage taken.

Success: Half damage only.
Life Drain.chat: [[lookup @name]] extends a hand toward the target, gathering necrotic power to draw out their life force.

Sunlight Sensitivity.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
Sunlight Sensitivity.description: While in sunlight, [[lookup @name]] has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

Charm Person.name: Charm Person
Charm Person.description:
One Humanoid you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. It does so with Advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. On a failed save, the target has the &Reference[charmed apply=false] condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Charmed.name: Charmed
Charmed.description: the target has the &Reference[charmed apply=false] condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

Fear.name: Fear
Fear.description:
Each creature in a 30-foot Cone must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or drop whatever it is holding and have the &Reference[frightened apply=false] condition for the duration.

A Frightened creature takes the &Reference[dash] action and moves away from you by the safest route on each of its turns unless there is nowhere to move. If the creature ends its turn in a space where it doesn't have line of sight to you, the creature makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, the spell ends on that creature.

Fear.name: Fear
Fear.description: A &Reference[frightened apply=false] creature takes the &Reference[dash] action and moves away from you by the safest route on each of its turns unless there is nowhere to move. If the creature ends its turn in a space where it doesn’t have line of sight to you, the creature makes a [[/save ability=wis]] saving throw. On a successful save, the spell ends on that creature.

Legendary Resistance.name: Legendary Resistance
Legendary Resistance.description: [[lookup @name]] fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Incorporeal Movement.name: Incorporeal Movement
Incorporeal Movement.description: [[lookup @name]] can move through other creatures and objects as if they were &Reference[Difficult Terrain]. It takes [[/damage average]] damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

Disintegrate.name: Disintegrate
Disintegrate.description:
You launch a green ray at a target you can see within range. The target can be a creature, a nonmagical object, or a creation of magical force, such as the wall created by @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.spells24.Item.phbsplWallofForc]{Wall of Force}.

A creature targeted by this spell makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 10d6 + 40 Force damage. If this damage reduces it to 0 Hit Points, it and everything nonmagical it is wearing and carrying are disintegrated into gray dust. The target can be revived only by a @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.spells24.Item.phbsplTrueResurr]{True Resurrection} or a @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.spells24.Item.phbsplWish000000]{Wish} spell.

This spell automatically disintegrates a Large or smaller nonmagical object or a creation of magical force. If such a target is Huge or larger, this spell disintegrates a 10-foot-Cube portion of it.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 3d6 for each spell slot level above 6.
Disintegrate.targetAffects: a creature, a nonmagical object, or a creation of magical force,

Multiattack.name: Multiattack
Multiattack.description: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] makes three [[/item Pseudopod]] attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of [[/item Suction Burst]] if available.

Pseudopod.name: Pseudopod
Pseudopod.description: [[/attack extended]]. [[/damage average extended]], and the creature has the &Reference[Grappled apply=false] condition (escape DC [[lookup @skills.ath.passive]]) from one of four pseudopods.
Pseudopod.chat: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] extends its pseudopod, delivering a powerful, grasping blow.

Grappled.name: Grappled
Grappled.description: The target is &Reference[grappled apply=false] by the creature and can use their action to Escape.

Suction Burst.name: Suction Burst
Suction Burst.description:
Strength Saving Throw: DC [[lookup @save.dc.value activity=3IpX2jcPVu1OXaVk]], one creature Grappled by the [[lookup @name lowercase]].

Failure: [[/damage average]] damage, and the target is repelled from the [[lookup @name lowercase]]. The target no longer has the Grappled condition, is pushed up to 30 feet straight away from the [[lookup @name lowercase]], and has the &Reference[Deafened apply=false] and &Reference[Stunned apply=false] conditions until the end of its next turn.

Success: Half damage only.

Deafened & Stunned.name: Deafened & Stunned
Deafened & Stunned.description: The target is &Reference[Deafened apply=false] and &Reference[Stunned apply=false] conditions until the end of its next turn.

Cold Sprint.name: Cold Sprint
Cold Sprint.description:
Trigger: [[lookup @activation.condition activity=OMTjacQKsF6gqO79]]

Response: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] moves up to its Speed and can make one [[/item Pseudopod]] attack with Advantage.

utility.condition: The shoggoth is subjected to Cold damage.
utility.activationCondition: The shoggoth is subjected to Cold damage.

Amphibious.name: Amphibious
Amphibious.description: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] can breathe air and water.

Susceptible to Charm.name: Susceptible to Charm
Susceptible to Charm.description: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] has Disadvantage on saving throws to avoid or end the &Reference[Charmed apply=false] condition.

Mimicry.name: Mimicry
Mimicry.description: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] can mimic simple sounds it has heard, such as a whisper or chitter. A hearer can discern the sounds are imitations with a successful [[/check format=long]].

Vampiric Longsword.name: Vampiric Longsword
Vampiric Longsword.description: [[/attack extended]]. [[/damage average extended]]. The target’s Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage taken.

Shield.name: Shield
Shield.description: A shield is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your Armor Class by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

Longsword.name: Longsword
Longsword.description: [[/attack extended]]. [[/damage average extended]]  or [[/damage twoHanded average]] damage if used with two hands.

Multiattack.name: Multiattack
Multiattack.description: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] makes two [[/item Vampiric Longsword]] attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of [[/item Shield Bash]].

Shield Bash.name: Shield Bash
Shield Bash.description:
Strength Saving Throw: DC [[lookup @save.dc.value activity=jYgzdBMWuj79j0Ri]], one creature within [[lookup @range.value activity=jYgzdBMWuj79j0Ri]] feet that the skeleton can see.

Failure: [[/damage average]] damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the &Reference[Prone apply=false] condition.

Prone.name: Prone
Prone.description: The target is knocked &Reference[prone apply=false] and can use half of their movement on their turn to stand up.

Undead Fortitude.name: Undead Fortitude
Undead Fortitude.description: If damage reduces the [[lookup @name lowercase]] to 0 Hit Points, it makes a Constitution saving throw (DC 5 plus the damage taken) unless the damage is Radiant or from a Critical Hit. On a successful save, the [[lookup @name lowercase]] drops to 1 Hit Point instead.

Spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
Spellcasting.description:
[[lookup @name]] casts one of the following spells, using [[lookup @attributes.spell.abilityLabel]] as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC [[lookup @attributes.spell.dc]]):

At Will: [[/item .27Y5kCgmRy0cEj4B]]{Detect Thoughts}, [[/item .nosskFoqkuFKz1vV]]{Fog Cloud}, [[/item .OB0OWFMofsh5QTQm]]{Mage Hand}

2/Day Each: [[/item .Se2ET6vjeBCUA86J]]{Animate Dead}, [[/item .koOE6spJKQbr3WHp]]{Fireball}, [[/item .ZxB3wDDXrccSVcdw]]{Gust of Wind}, [[/item .gTHoIwssRWitwJEK]]{Mirror Image}, [[/item .j34OdYfzkjAz85PJ]]{Nondetection}

1/Day Each: [[/item .h4sjhJxwOHiMxI4V]]{Greater Invisibility}, [[/item .mUvAMjH0XWDv9i8R]]{Polymorph}, [[/item .nLCsoYQfTYuPmhCy]]{Scrying}

Multiattack.name: Multiattack
Multiattack.description: Strahd makes two [[/item Death Strike]] attacks. He can replace one of these attacks with a use of (A) [[/item .vZnptBVI5hbc5vMI]]{Bite} or (B) [[/item Spellcasting]] to cast [[/item Spellcasting activity="Fireball"]]{Fireball} if available.

Bite (Bat or Vampire Form Only).name: Bite (Bat or Vampire Form Only)
Bite (Bat or Vampire Form Only).description:
Constitution Saving Throw: DC [[lookup @save.dc.value activity=1NiuoT80BC9X8nuN]], one creature within 5 feet that is willing or that has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition.

Failure: [[/damage average]] damage. The target’s Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage taken, and [[lookup @name]] regains Hit Points equal to that amount.

A Humanoid reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage rises the following night as a @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.monsters.Actor.zpaZb7I8alY32vWV]{Vampire Spawn} under [[lookup @name]]’s control, unless the Humanoid is restored to life or its body is destroyed.
Bite (Bat or Vampire Form Only).chat: [[lookup @name]] pulls the target close and leans in to bite.

Count’s Command.name: Count’s Command
Count’s Command.description: [[lookup @name]] casts [[/item .Re17z5AXXFXDajkv]]{Command} (level 2 version), requiring no spell components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
Count’s Command.chat: [[lookup @name]] fixes his eyes on a target with his gaze and delivers a single, forceful order.

Charm.name: Charm
Charm.description:
[[lookup @name]] casts [[/item .UHcSjddcu7h13w34]]{Charm Person}, requiring no spell components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

When the spell is cast this way, its duration is 24 hours, and the &Reference[Charmed apply=false] target is a willing recipient of [[lookup @name]]’s Bite, the damage of which doesn’t end the spell. When the spell ends, the target is unaware it was Charmed by [[lookup @name]].
Charm.chat: [[lookup @name]] fixes the target with a steady gaze and speaks in a low, persuasive voice.

Death Strike.name: Death Strike
Death Strike.description:
[[/attack extended]]. [[/damage average extended]].

If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the &Reference[Grappled apply=false] condition (escape DC [[lookup @skills.ath.passive]]) from one of two hands.
Death Strike.chat: Strahd steps in with precision, slashing at the target and reaching to seize them with his free hand.

Grappled.name: Grappled
Grappled.description: The target is &Reference[grappled apply=false] by Strahd and can use their action to Escape.

Shape-Shift.name: Shape-Shift
Shape-Shift.description:
If [[lookup @name]] isn’t in sunlight or running water, he shape-shifts into a Tiny bat (Speed 5 ft., Fly Speed 30 ft.), a Medium wolf (Speed 40 ft.), or a Medium cloud of mist (Speed 5 ft., Fly Speed 20 ft. [hover]), or he returns to his vampire form. Anything he is wearing transforms with him.

While in bat or wolf form, [[lookup @name]] can’t speak. His game statistics, other than his size and Speed, are unchanged.

While in mist form, [[lookup @name]] can’t take any actions, speak, or manipulate objects. He is weightless and can enter an enemy’s space and stop there. If air can pass through a space, [[lookup @name]] can do so, but he can’t pass through liquid. [[lookup @name]] has Resistance to all damage, except the damage he takes from sunlight.
Shape-Shift.chat: [[lookup @name]]’s form begins to shift, his outline changing with unnatural control.

Misty Escape.name: Misty Escape
Misty Escape.description:
If [[lookup @name]] drops to 0 Hit Points outside his resting place, he uses [[/item Shape-Shift]] to become mist (no action required). If he can’t use Shape-Shift, he is destroyed.

While [[lookup @name]] has 0 Hit Points in mist form, he can’t return to his vampire form, and he must reach his resting place within 2 hours or be destroyed.

Once in his resting place, [[lookup @name]] returns to his vampire form and has the &Reference[Paralyzed apply=false] condition until he regains any Hit Points, and he regains 1 Hit Point after spending 1 hour there.

Paralyzed.name: Paralyzed
Paralyzed.description: Strahd is &Reference[Paralyzed apply=false] condition until he regains any Hit Points, and he regains 1 Hit Point after spending 1 hour there.

Darklord Restoration.name: Darklord Restoration
Darklord Restoration.description: If [[lookup @name]] dies, he revives with all his Hit Points in [[/r 1d10]] weeks somewhere in Barovia.

Striding Strike.name: Striding Strike
Striding Strike.description: [[lookup @name]] moves up to half his Speed, and he makes one [[/item Death Strike]] attack.
Striding Strike.chat: [[lookup @name]] advances in a smooth, deliberate motion and makes a cutting strike.

Command.name: Command
Command.description:
You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. Choose the command from these options:

Approach. The target moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 5 feet of you.

Drop. The target drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn.

Flee. The target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means.

Grovel. The target has the Prone condition and then ends its turn.

Halt. On its turn, the target doesn't move and takes no action or Bonus Action.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can affect one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Charm Person.name: Charm Person
Charm Person.description:
One Humanoid you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. It does so with Advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. On a failed save, the target has the &Reference[charmed apply=false] condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Charmed.name: Charmed
Charmed.description: the target has the &Reference[charmed apply=false] condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

Detect Thoughts.name: Detect Thoughts
Detect Thoughts.description:
You activate one of the effects below. Until the spell ends, you can activate either effect as a Magic action on your later turns.

Sense Thoughts. You sense the presence of thoughts within 30 feet of yourself that belong to creatures that know languages or are telepathic. You don't read the thoughts, but you know that a thinking creature is present.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

Read Thoughts. Target one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself or one creature within 30 feet of yourself that you detected with the Sense Thoughts option. You learn what is most on the target's mind right now. If the target doesn't know any languages and isn't telepathic, you learn nothing.

As a Magic action on your next turn, you can try to probe deeper into the target's mind. If you probe deeper, the target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, you discern the target's reasoning, emotions, and something that looms large in its mind (such as a worry, love, or hate). On a successful save, the spell ends. Either way, the target knows that you are probing into its mind, and until you shift your attention away from the target's mind, the target can take an action on its turn to make an [[/check ability=int skill=arc dc=@attributes.spell.dc]] check against your spell save DC, ending the spell on a success.

Fog Cloud.name: Fog Cloud
Fog Cloud.description:
You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of fog centered on a point within range. The Sphere is &Reference[heavilyobscured]. It lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as one created by @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.spells24.Item.phbsplGustofWind]{Gust of Wind}) disperses it.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The fog's radius increases by 20 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

Mage Hand.name: Mage Hand
Mage Hand.description:
A spectral, floating hand appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for the duration. The hand vanishes if it is ever more than 30 feet away from you or if you cast this spell again.

When you cast the spell, you can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial.

As a Magic action on your later turns, you can control the hand thus again. As part of that action, you can move the hand up to 30 feet.

The hand can't attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds.

Gust of Wind.name: Gust of Wind
Gust of Wind.description:
A Line of strong wind 60 feet long and 10 feet wide blasts from you in a direction you choose for the duration. Each creature in the Line must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away from you in a direction following the Line. A creature that ends its turn in the Line must make the same save.

Any creature in the Line must spend 2 feet of movement for every 1 foot it moves when moving closer to you.

The gust disperses gas or vapor, and it extinguishes candles and similar unprotected flames in the area. It causes protected flames, such as those of lanterns, to dance wildly and has a 50 percent chance to extinguish them.

As a Bonus Action on your later turns, you can change the direction in which the Line blasts from you.

Fireball.name: Fireball
Fireball.description:
A bright streak flashes from you to a point you choose within range and then blossoms with a low roar into a fiery explosion. [[lookup @labels.description.affects capitalize]] in a [[lookup @labels.description.template]] centered on that point makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried start burning.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 3.
Fireball.materials: a ball of bat guano and sulfur

Animate Dead.name: Animate Dead
Animate Dead.description:
Choose a pile of bones or a corpse of a Medium or Small Humanoid within range. The target becomes an Undead creature: a @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.actors24.Actor.mmSkeleton000000]{Skeleton} if you chose bones or a @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.actors24.Actor.mmZombie00000000]{Zombie} if you chose a corpse.

On each of your turns, you can take a Bonus Action to mentally command any creature you made with this spell if the creature is within 60 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move on its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a chamber or corridor. If you issue no commands, the creature takes the Dodge action and moves only to avoid harm. Once given an order, the creature continues to follow it until its task is complete.

The creature is under your control for 24 hours, after which it stops obeying any command you've given it. To maintain control of the creature for another 24 hours, you must cast this spell on the creature again before the current 24-hour period ends. This use of the spell reasserts your control over up to four creatures you have animated with this spell rather than animating a new creature.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You animate or reassert control over two additional Undead creatures for each spell slot level above 3. Each of the creatures must come from a different corpse or pile of bones.
Animate Dead.materials: a drop of blood, a piece of flesh, and a pinch of bone dust

Mirror Image.name: Mirror Image
Mirror Image.description:
Three illusory duplicates of yourself appear in your space. Until the spell ends, the duplicates move with you and mimic your actions, shifting position so it's impossible to track which image is real.

Each time a creature hits you with an attack roll during the spell's duration, roll a [[/r (@flags.dnd-players-handbook.mirrorImages)d6kh1cs>2#Duplicate destroyed]]{d6 for each} of your remaining duplicates. If any of the d6s rolls a 3 or higher, one of the duplicates is hit instead of you, and the duplicate is destroyed. The duplicates otherwise ignore all other damage and effects. The spell ends when all three duplicates are destroyed.

A creature is unaffected by this spell if it has the &Reference[blinded apply=false] condition, &Reference[blindsight], or &Reference[truesight].

Nondetection.name: Nondetection
Nondetection.description: For the duration, you hide a target that you touch from Divination spells. The target can be a willing creature, or it can be a place or an object no larger than 10 feet in any dimension. The target can't be targeted by any Divination spell or perceived through magical scrying sensors.
Nondetection.materials: a pinch of diamond dust worth 25+ GP, which the spell consumes

Polymorph.name: Polymorph
Polymorph.description:
You attempt to transform a creature that you can see within range into a Beast. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or shape-shift into a Beast form for the duration. That form can be any Beast you choose that has a Challenge Rating equal to or less than the target's (or the target's level if it doesn't have a Challenge Rating). The target's game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the chosen Beast, but the target retains its alignment, personality, creature type, Hit Points, and Hit Point Dice. See appendix B for a sample of Beast stat blocks.

The target gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the Hit Points of the Beast form. These Temporary Hit Points vanish if any remain when the spell ends. The spell ends early on the target if it has no Temporary Hit Points left.

The target is limited in the actions it can perform by the anatomy of its new form, and it can't speak or cast spells.

The target's gear melds into the new form. The creature can't use or otherwise benefit from any of that equipment.

Greater Invisibility.name: Greater Invisibility
Greater Invisibility.description: A creature you touch has the Invisible condition until the spell ends.

Scrying.name: Scrying
Scrying.description:
You can see and hear a creature you choose that is on the same plane of existence as you. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw, which is modified (see the tables below) by how well you know the target and the sort of physical connection you have to it. The target doesn't know what it is making the save against, only that it feels uneasy.

Your Knowledge of the Target Is …Save Modifier
Secondhand (heard of the target)+5
Firsthand (met the target)+0
Extensive (know the target well)−5
You Have the Target's …Save Modifier
Picture or other likeness−2
Garment or other possession−4
Body part, lock of hair, or bit of nail−10

On a successful save, the target isn't affected, and you can't use this spell on it again for 24 hours.

On a failed save, the spell creates an Invisible, intangible sensor within 10 feet of the target. You can see and hear through the sensor as if you were there. The sensor moves with the target, remaining within 10 feet of it for the duration. If something can see the sensor, it appears as a luminous orb about the size of your fist.

Instead of targeting a creature, you can target a location you have seen. When you do so, the sensor appears at that location and doesn't move.
Scrying.materials: a focus worth 1,000+ GP, such as a crystal ball, mirror, or water-filled font
Scrying.targetAffects: creature on same plane of existence or a location you have seen

Create Sensor.name: Create Sensor
Create Sensor.target: at target location or within 10 feet of target creature
Create Sensor.targetAffects: at target location or within 10 feet of target creature

Legendary Resistance.name: Legendary Resistance
Legendary Resistance.description: If the [[lookup @name]] fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

Spider Climb.name: Spider Climb
Spider Climb.description: [[lookup @name]] can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Vampire Weakness.name: Vampire Weakness
Vampire Weakness.description:
[[lookup @name]] has these weaknesses:

Forbiddance. [[lookup @name]] can’t enter a residence without an invitation from an occupant.

Running Water. [[lookup @name]] takes [[/damage activity=Ss5LwXo6bHOciPNQ]] damage if it ends its turn in running water.

Stake to the Heart. If a weapon that deals Piercing damage is driven into [[lookup @name]]’s heart while [[lookup @name]] has the Incapacitated condition in his resting place, [[lookup @name]] has the &Reference[Paralyzed apply=false] condition until the weapon is removed.

Sunlight. [[lookup @name]] takes [[/damage activity=Q18pvRsbOyRbHiVK]] damage if he starts his turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, he has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

Disadv. Attacks & Checks.name: Disadv. Attacks & Checks
Disadv. Attacks & Checks.description: While in sunlight, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

Paralyzed.name: Paralyzed
Paralyzed.description: Strahd has the &Reference[Paralyzed apply=false] condition until the weapon that deals Piercing damage is removed.

gEBI6m4U4ZQYn9OL.name: Charm Person
gEBI6m4U4ZQYn9OL.description:
One Humanoid you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. It does so with Advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. On a failed save, the target has the &Reference[charmed apply=false] condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Charmed.name: Charmed
Charmed.description: the target has the &Reference[charmed apply=false] condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

Multiattack.name: Multiattack
Multiattack.description: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] makes two [[/item Claw]] attacks and one [[/item Proboscis]] attack.

Claw.name: Claw
Claw.description: Melee Attack Roll: [[/attack]], reach 5 ft. Hit: [[/damage average]] damage.
Claw.chat: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] swipes its target with its twisted claw.

Proboscis.name: Proboscis
Proboscis.description: [[/attack extended]]. [[/damage average extended]]. The target’s Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage taken, and the [[lookup @name lowercase]] regains Hit Points equal to that amount. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this attack dies, leaving behind nothing except its skin and equipment.
Proboscis.chat: The [[lookup @name lowercase]] jabs its long proboscis into its target.

Protective Swarm.name: Protective Swarm
Protective Swarm.description:

Whenever the swarm enters a creature’s space or a creature enters the swarm or ends its turn there, that creature is subjected to the following effect (a creature makes this save only once per turn, and strigoi and stirges are unaffected).

Constitution Saving Throw: DC [[lookup @save.dc.value activity=5RjKNUuZHTVrB7pV]].

Failure: [[/damage average]] damage, and the target has the &Reference[Poisoned apply=false] condition until the end of its next turn.
Protective Swarm.chat: A swarm of mosquitoes forms around the strigoi.

Unspeakable Horror.name: Unspeakable Horror
Unspeakable Horror.description:
Terrifying Monster of the Mists

Habitat: Any; Treasure: None

Unspeakable horrors are anathema to all other life in the multiverse. Their shapes change constantly in ways that defy and disrupt reality. Those who look on these beings instantly understand their absolute and awful wrongness. Such revelations are often the last witnesses have before they’re dissolved by discordant, destructive forces.

Multiattack.description: The horror makes three [[/item Phantasmic Assault]] attacks.

Phantasmic Assault.name: Phantasmic Assault

Warp Mind.chat: The horror seeks to disrupt its target's focus by sending a pulse of psychic energy toward the creature's mind.

Animate Objects.name: Animate Objects
Animate Objects.description:
Objects animate at your command. Choose a number of nonmagical objects within range that aren't being worn or carried, aren't fixed to a surface, and aren't Gargantuan. The maximum number of objects is equal to your spellcasting ability modifier; for this number, a Medium or smaller target counts as one object, a Large target counts as two, and a Huge target counts as three.

Each target animates, sprouts legs, and becomes a Construct that uses the Animated Object stat block; this creature is under your control until the spell ends or until it is reduced to 0 Hit Points. Each creature you make with this spell is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, it shares your Initiative count and takes its turn immediately after yours.

Until the spell ends, you can take a Bonus Action to mentally command any creature you made with this spell if the creature is within 500 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). If you issue no commands, the creature takes the &Reference[Dodge] action and moves only to avoid harm. When the creature drops to 0 Hit Points, it reverts to its object form, and any remaining damage carries over to that form.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The creature's Slam damage increases by 1d4 (Medium or smaller), 1d6 (Large), or 1d12 (Huge) for each spell slot level above 5.

The [[lookup @name lowercase]] now controls the body; the target has no awareness of the [[lookup @name lowercase]]’s actions.

The [[lookup @name lowercase]] can’t be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect. The [[lookup @name lowercase]]’s game statistics are the same, except it uses the possessed target’s Speed and Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution modifiers. It knows everything the target knew.

The possession lasts until the body drops to 0 Hit Points or the [[lookup @name lowercase]] leaves as a Bonus Action. When the possession ends, the [[lookup @name lowercase]] returns to its body, which appears within 5 feet of the target. The target is &Reference[Stunned apply=false] for 1 minute by alien memories, and it is immune to this [[lookup @name lowercase]]’s Mind Swap for 24 hours.

Clairvoyance.name: Clairvoyance
Clairvoyance.description:
You create an Invisible sensor within range in a location familiar to you (a place you have visited or seen before) or in an obvious location that is unfamiliar to you (such as behind a door, around a corner, or in a grove of trees). The intangible, invulnerable sensor remains in place for the duration.

When you cast the spell, choose seeing or hearing. You can use the chosen sense through the sensor as if you were in its space. As a Bonus Action, you can switch between seeing and hearing.

A creature that sees the sensor (such as a creature benefiting from See Invisibility or &Reference[truesight]) sees a luminous orb about the size of your fist.
Clairvoyance.materials: a focus worth 100+ GP, either a jeweled horn for hearing or a glass eye for seeing

Modify Memory.name: Modify Memory
Modify Memory.description:
You attempt to reshape another creature's memories. One creature that you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. If you are fighting the creature, it has Advantage on the save. On a failed save, the target has the &Reference[Charmed apply=false] condition for the duration. While Charmed in this way, the target also has the &Reference[Incapacitated apply=false] condition and is unaware of its surroundings, though it can hear you. If it takes any damage or is targeted by another spell, this spell ends, and no memories are modified.

While this charm lasts, you can affect the target's memory of an event that it experienced within the last 24 hours and that lasted no more than 10 minutes. You can permanently eliminate all memory of the event, allow the target to recall the event with perfect clarity, change its memory of the event's details, or create a memory of some other event.

You must speak to the target to describe how its memories are affected, and it must be able to understand your language for the modified memories to take root. Its mind fills in any gaps in the details of your description. If the spell ends before you finish describing the modified memories, the creature's memory isn't altered. Otherwise, the modified memories take hold when the spell ends.

A modified memory doesn't necessarily affect how a creature behaves, particularly if the memory contradicts the creature's natural inclinations, alignment, or beliefs. An illogical modified memory, such as a false memory of how much the creature enjoyed swimming in acid, is dismissed as a bad dream. The DM might deem a modified memory too nonsensical to affect a creature.

A @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.spells24.Item.phbsplRemoveCurs]{Remove Curse} or @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.spells24.Item.phbsplGreaterRes]{Greater Restoration} spell cast on the target restores the creature's true memory.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can alter the target's memories of an event that took place up to 7 days ago (level 6 spell slot), 30 days ago (level 7 spell slot), 365 days ago (level 8 spell slot), or any time in the creature's past (level 9 spell slot).

Transport via Plants.name: Transport via Plants
Transport via Plants.description: This spell creates a magical link between a Large or larger inanimate plant within range and another plant, at any distance, on the same plane of existence. You must have seen or touched the destination plant at least once before. For the duration, any creature can step into the target plant and exit from the destination plant by using 5 feet of movement.
